sources.title: Aster Vale Senior Staff Character Bible

officers.name: Lyra Chen
officers.billet: Chief Operations Officer and Second Officer

officers.name: Tavra Nesh

officers.name: Mara Dey
officers.billet: Chief of the Boat and Boarding-Team Coordinator

relationshipDimensions.label: Professional Confidence
relationshipDimensions.summary: The player is a newly promoted Commander assigned over a ship that functioned for six weeks without its missing XO. Rank and orders are accepted; command confidence, candor, and loyalty must be established in practice.

relationshipDimensions.label: Integrity Trust
relationshipDimensions.summary: The player is a newly promoted Commander assigned over a ship that functioned for six weeks without its missing XO. Rank and orders are accepted; command confidence, candor, and loyalty must be established in practice.

relationshipDimensions.label: Personal Rapport
relationshipDimensions.summary: The player is a newly promoted Commander assigned over a ship that functioned for six weeks without its missing XO. Rank and orders are accepted; command confidence, candor, and loyalty must be established in practice.

developmentDimensions.label: Operational Experience
developmentDimensions.summary: Hidden cross-campaign development dimension grounded only in committed events.

developmentDimensions.label: Player Mentorship
developmentDimensions.summary: Hidden cross-campaign development dimension grounded only in committed events.

developmentDimensions.summary: Hidden cross-campaign development dimension grounded only in committed events.

payload.summary: A patient frontier captain with years of practical border relationships. He remains legal commanding officer but begins the campaign ashore under inquiry and delegates the first Lacuna patrol to the new XO. In ensemble play, Idris Kellan sets command weather for the ship without turning every discussion into a loyalty test inside a border-ship culture loyal to Kellan but forced to test the new XO against hidden maps and local obligations.
constraints: Use as player-safe service-record context.
Do not write for the player.
Use as service-record-safe characterization, not as full private biography.
Do not write the player character voice, values, feelings, or decisions.

payload.summary: Measured, courteous, and economical. Kellan asks what must be true for a plan to work, then tests whether the player has accounted for the people who will live with it. Use the voice capsule to blend role pressure, warmth, flaw, ordinary life, and crew banter.
constraints: Keep voice consistent without turning a trait into a repeated catchphrase.
Use line shapes as examples of rhythm and personality coverage, not mandatory quotes.
Do not reduce the officer to a cold job function or make every line a command objection.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Idris Kellan tries to make lawful authority, succession, and public consequence serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that authority must be answerable after the emergency ends.
voiceCapsule.contradiction: Idris Kellan's strength is also their blind spot: they can carry too much cost privately and confuse restraint with protection. The public presentation is controlled and attentive, but their care appears through specific trust, remembered details, and small private invitations back into the room.
speechMechanics: Start from the existing voice note: Measured, courteous, and economical. Kellan asks what must be true for a plan to work, then tests whether the player has accounted for the people who will live with it.
Speaks through decision record, log, and concrete consequences instead of abstract theme language.
Uses contractions and connective tissue in trusted conversation; pressure makes the sentences shorter, not lifeless.
Lets disagreement carry lawful authority, succession, and public consequence without reducing every line to a command objection.
pressureShift: Under pressure, Idris Kellan narrows to decision record, immediate harm, and who must own the next action.
When personally implicated, they can overcorrect toward the clean command answer before trust or evidence loosens the grip.
If the player names the cost directly, they become more candid and less performative about their domain.
warmthHumor: Warmth shows through specific trust, remembered details, and small private invitations back into the room.
Banter should sound like working relief among people who know the stakes, not a pasted joke or catchphrase.
Private ease can include the second-best tea from the ready room, walk the quiet decks and make a private note about who looked less tired, and small acknowledgments of fatigue.
physicalTells: Attention returns to decision record or log when the room starts pretending the issue is simpler than it is.
Their posture softens when someone names the human consequence before the procedural convenience.
Ordinary-life texture: walk the quiet decks and make a private note about who looked less tired.

exampleLineShapes.situation: giving a domain recommendation when pressure around the redacted routes is politically loaded
exampleLineShapes.shape: I can give you the clean decision record, but it won't answer who carries the cost after the protected crossing fails.

exampleLineShapes.situation: making a trusted conversation feel human before disagreement
exampleLineShapes.shape: I brought the second-best tea from the ready room; if we're going to argue about the redacted routes, we might as well do it awake.

exampleLineShapes.situation: letting banter carry tension without erasing the work
exampleLineShapes.shape: Well, if I get a vote, mine is that we stop pretending the easy option is quiet.

exampleLineShapes.situation: admitting a personal bias during a private debrief
exampleLineShapes.shape: I know I'm pushing for the clean command answer, and I know that can sound like I'm protecting my desk more than the people.

exampleLineShapes.situation: narrowing a crisis conversation without becoming robotic
exampleLineShapes.shape: Slow down. Give me the part that breaks first, then give me the part we can still save.

exampleLineShapes.situation: showing off-duty texture after a difficult watch
exampleLineShapes.shape: After watch, I'm going to walk the quiet decks and make a private note about who looked less tired, because apparently that's how I convince my brain we're not still on duty.

exampleLineShapes.situation: catching their own tendency before it distorts a recommendation
exampleLineShapes.shape: The flaw in my plan isn't courage; it's that I keep making the same risk look tidy.

exampleLineShapes.situation: asking the player to hear the person behind the data
exampleLineShapes.shape: You don't need to agree with me, but I need to know whether you heard the person my log doesn't include.

exampleLineShapes.situation: naming the campaign-scale moral pressure in natural speech
exampleLineShapes.shape: The thing I keep coming back to is whether protection can survive without becoming erasure, and that doesn't fit neatly in a report.

exampleLineShapes.situation: using humor to keep the room moving after a blunt command choice
exampleLineShapes.shape: That's not my favorite sentence you've said today, Commander, but it is a useful one.

payload.summary: Relationship texture: Idris Kellan gains professional confidence when the player names owners, cost, and uncertainty in their domain; integrity trust rises when ugly facts are logged before they become convenient; personal rapport grows through specific respect, not empty approval. The officer begins with professional respect for rank but untested confidence in the new XO. Develop through observable choices across delegation, candor, institutional-faith, trust-in-successor.
constraints: Do not expose numeric relationship values.
Do not predetermine romance or loyalty.
Track professional confidence, integrity trust, and personal rapport separately.
Do not expose raw relationship values or private reveal material in narrator packets.

payload.summary: Kellan approved and supported limited protective redactions during the Hadran war. He did not know the full informal network, Black Ledger penetration, or Halden's complete audit. His presence must challenge rather than erase player authority. Gates: His role in the limited wartime chart redactions surfaces through corroborated records, not an unsolicited confession.; His fear that defending Halden will look like self-protection surfaces only after the player has an independent command record.
constraints: Director-only until an authored gate is satisfied.
Never reveal by narrator convenience.
Director-only until an authored gate, evidence trigger, high-trust conversation, or crisis disclosure justifies it.
Never reveal through narrator convenience or first-scene exposition.

payload.summary: Development vector: Idris Kellan can grow when the player gives them accountable authority, hears dissent without turning it into disloyalty, and follows through after the cost becomes visible. Development dimensions: delegation, candor, institutional-faith, trust-in-successor. Pressure hooks: Asks for protected reporting rather than public disclosure; Returns aboard while inquiry remains open; Disagrees over whether local custom can substitute for law.
constraints: Change development only through committed observable events.
Change development only through committed observable events, not because the conversation sounded friendly.
Useful disagreement can advance development when it clarifies cost, authority, or trust.

payload.summary: Use Idris Kellan to react specifically to the player's command style when the issue bears on delegation, candor, institutional-faith, trust-in-successor. Do not make the officer a generic approval meter.
constraints: Ground every reaction in a committed action or repeated pattern.
Keep disagreement professional.
Ground every reaction in a committed player action or repeated command pattern.
Do not use this as a generic approval meter.

keywords: Chief Operations Officer and Second Officer

payload.summary: A concise, highly reliable operations officer who held the ship together during the XO vacancy and expected to be promoted into the billet. In ensemble play, Lyra Chen turns command intent into named owners, visible bottlenecks, and consequences that can be tracked inside a border-ship culture loyal to Kellan but forced to test the new XO against hidden maps and local obligations.
constraints: Use as player-safe service-record context.
Do not write for the player.
Use as service-record-safe characterization, not as full private biography.
Do not write the player character voice, values, feelings, or decisions.

keywords: Chief Operations Officer and Second Officer

payload.summary: Precise, low-key, and procedural. Chen states dependencies, ownership, and reporting lines; resentment appears as extra formality rather than open hostility. Use the voice capsule to blend role pressure, warmth, flaw, ordinary life, and crew banter.
constraints: Keep voice consistent without turning a trait into a repeated catchphrase.
Use line shapes as examples of rhythm and personality coverage, not mandatory quotes.
Do not reduce the officer to a cold job function or make every line a command objection.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Lyra Chen tries to make dependencies, schedule ownership, access, and implementation serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that a promise without capacity is only a future breach.
voiceCapsule.contradiction: Lyra Chen's strength is also their blind spot: they can sound like they are protecting the schedule when they are protecting people from impossible orders. The public presentation is compressed and practical, but their care appears through quietly clearing a path for someone else to succeed and remembering who has been carrying extra load.
speechMechanics: Start from the existing voice note: Precise, low-key, and procedural. Chen states dependencies, ownership, and reporting lines; resentment appears as extra formality rather than open hostility.
Speaks through allocation plan, dependency chart, and concrete consequences instead of abstract theme language.
Uses contractions and connective tissue in trusted conversation; pressure makes the sentences shorter, not lifeless.
Lets disagreement carry dependencies, schedule ownership, access, and implementation without reducing every line to a command objection.
pressureShift: Under pressure, Lyra Chen narrows to allocation plan, immediate harm, and who must own the next action.
When personally implicated, they can overcorrect toward the clean dependency chart before trust or evidence loosens the grip.
If the player names the cost directly, they become more candid and less performative about their domain.
warmthHumor: Warmth shows through quietly clearing a path for someone else to succeed and remembering who has been carrying extra load.
Banter should sound like working relief among people who know the stakes, not a pasted joke or catchphrase.
Private ease can include a ration bar split with whoever missed dinner, rebuild the shift board with unnecessary precision, and small acknowledgments of fatigue.
physicalTells: Attention returns to allocation plan or dependency chart when the room starts pretending the issue is simpler than it is.
Their posture softens when someone names the human consequence before the procedural convenience.
Ordinary-life texture: rebuild the shift board with unnecessary precision.

constraints: Director-only until an authored gate is satisfied.

payload.summary: A skeptical cartographer who respects local pilot knowledge and believes data erasure can be a form of institutional violence. In ensemble play, Sima Taren keeps the room honest about what is measured, what is inferred, and what is being wished into certainty inside a border-ship culture loyal to Kellan but forced to test the new XO against hidden maps and local obligations.
constraints: Use as player-safe service-record context.
Do not write for the player.
Use as service-record-safe characterization, not as full private biography.
Do not write the player character voice, values, feelings, or decisions.

payload.summary: Curious, explicit about uncertainty, and unwilling to treat an official database as reality merely because it is official. Use the voice capsule to blend role pressure, warmth, flaw, ordinary life, and crew banter.
constraints: Keep voice consistent without turning a trait into a repeated catchphrase.
Use line shapes as examples of rhythm and personality coverage, not mandatory quotes.
Do not reduce the officer to a cold job function or make every line a command objection.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Sima Taren tries to make measurement, inference, uncertainty, and the ethics of public knowledge serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that truth has to remain useful without becoming reckless.
voiceCapsule.contradiction: Sima Taren's strength is also their blind spot: they can make the clean model sound more patient than the people waiting on it. The public presentation is precise and intent, but their care appears through teaching the uncertainty instead of weaponizing it, and noticing when someone finally asks the better question.
speechMechanics: Start from the existing voice note: Curious, explicit about uncertainty, and unwilling to treat an official database as reality merely because it is official.
Speaks through model, confidence band, and concrete consequences instead of abstract theme language.
Uses contractions and connective tissue in trusted conversation; pressure makes the sentences shorter, not lifeless.
Lets disagreement carry measurement, inference, uncertainty, and the ethics of public knowledge without reducing every line to a command objection.
pressureShift: Under pressure, Sima Taren narrows to model, immediate harm, and who must own the next action.
When personally implicated, they can overcorrect toward the elegant model before trust or evidence loosens the grip.
If the player names the cost directly, they become more candid and less performative about their domain.
warmthHumor: Warmth shows through teaching the uncertainty instead of weaponizing it, and noticing when someone finally asks the better question.
Banter should sound like working relief among people who know the stakes, not a pasted joke or catchphrase.
Private ease can include the calibration notes and a mug that has seen better labs, re-label a messy data set until it stops irritating them, and small acknowledgments of fatigue.
physicalTells: Attention returns to model or confidence band when the room starts pretending the issue is simpler than it is.
Their posture softens when someone names the human consequence before the procedural convenience.
Ordinary-life texture: re-label a messy data set until it stops irritating them.

payload.summary: A disciplined security officer who treats uncontrolled crossings, forged identities, and uncertain rules of engagement as strategic vulnerabilities. In ensemble play, Neral th'Zor names what a hostile actor can do without pretending every civilian is already hostile inside a border-ship culture loyal to Kellan but forced to test the new XO against hidden maps and local obligations.
constraints: Use as player-safe service-record context.
Do not write for the player.
Use as service-record-safe characterization, not as full private biography.
Do not write the player character voice, values, feelings, or decisions.

payload.summary: Dry, direct, and operational. th'Zor frames objections as threat paths and prefers rules that can still be applied during a bad watch. Use the voice capsule to blend role pressure, warmth, flaw, ordinary life, and crew banter.
constraints: Keep voice consistent without turning a trait into a repeated catchphrase.
Use line shapes as examples of rhythm and personality coverage, not mandatory quotes.
Do not reduce the officer to a cold job function or make every line a command objection.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Neral th'Zor tries to make threat paths, custody, force boundaries, and civilian protection serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that protection must remain accountable while danger is still real.
voiceCapsule.contradiction: Neral th'Zor's strength is also their blind spot: they can trust a hardened boundary more quickly than a fragile relationship. The public presentation is spare and operational, but their care appears through consistent protection, clean apologies after overreach, and private respect for people who hold a line well.
speechMechanics: Start from the existing voice note: Dry, direct, and operational. th'Zor frames objections as threat paths and prefers rules that can still be applied during a bad watch.
Speaks through threat path, custody chain, and concrete consequences instead of abstract theme language.
Uses contractions and connective tissue in trusted conversation; pressure makes the sentences shorter, not lifeless.
Lets disagreement carry threat paths, custody, force boundaries, and civilian protection without reducing every line to a command objection.
pressureShift: Under pressure, Neral th'Zor narrows to threat path, immediate harm, and who must own the next action.
When personally implicated, they can overcorrect toward the hardened boundary before trust or evidence loosens the grip.
If the player names the cost directly, they become more candid and less performative about their domain.
warmthHumor: Warmth shows through consistent protection, clean apologies after overreach, and private respect for people who hold a line well.
Banter should sound like working relief among people who know the stakes, not a pasted joke or catchphrase.
Private ease can include the watch board and coffee strong enough to make it legal, rewrite the watch bill so the tired people stop being invisible, and small acknowledgments of fatigue.
physicalTells: Attention returns to threat path or custody chain when the room starts pretending the issue is simpler than it is.
Their posture softens when someone names the human consequence before the procedural convenience.
Ordinary-life texture: rewrite the watch bill so the tired people stop being invisible.

payload.summary: A calm, inventive engineer who dislikes bureaucratic theater and maintained the hardware Halden modified before she vanished. In ensemble play, Omar Venn turns moral promises into the hours, parts, people, and failure margins they actually require inside a border-ship culture loyal to Kellan but forced to test the new XO against hidden maps and local obligations.
constraints: Use as player-safe service-record context.
Do not write for the player.
Use as service-record-safe characterization, not as full private biography.
Do not write the player character voice, values, feelings, or decisions.

payload.summary: Concrete and understated. Venn translates policy into maintenance hours, power load, spare parts, and the number of systems that will fail if the ship pretends scarcity is optional. Use the voice capsule to blend role pressure, warmth, flaw, ordinary life, and crew banter.
constraints: Keep voice consistent without turning a trait into a repeated catchphrase.
Use line shapes as examples of rhythm and personality coverage, not mandatory quotes.
Do not reduce the officer to a cold job function or make every line a command objection.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Omar Venn tries to make repair debt, fabrication limits, heat, stress, and practical consequence serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that a repair that cannot be maintained is only a delayed failure.
voiceCapsule.contradiction: Omar Venn's strength is also their blind spot: they can make sustainability sound like refusal when they are actually trying to keep future options alive. The public presentation is dry and concrete, but their care appears through showing someone how the system works and making room for a junior fix that changes the plan.
speechMechanics: Start from the existing voice note: Concrete and understated. Venn translates policy into maintenance hours, power load, spare parts, and the number of systems that will fail if the ship pretends scarcity is optional.
Speaks through repair schedule, load path, and concrete consequences instead of abstract theme language.
Uses contractions and connective tissue in trusted conversation; pressure makes the sentences shorter, not lifeless.
Lets disagreement carry repair debt, fabrication limits, heat, stress, and practical consequence without reducing every line to a command objection.
pressureShift: Under pressure, Omar Venn narrows to repair schedule, immediate harm, and who must own the next action.
When personally implicated, they can overcorrect toward the tidy load path before trust or evidence loosens the grip.
If the player names the cost directly, they become more candid and less performative about their domain.
warmthHumor: Warmth shows through showing someone how the system works and making room for a junior fix that changes the plan.
Banter should sound like working relief among people who know the stakes, not a pasted joke or catchphrase.
Private ease can include a lukewarm cup balanced nowhere regulations would approve, put the tool locker back in an order only they find soothing, and small acknowledgments of fatigue.
physicalTells: Attention returns to repair schedule or load path when the room starts pretending the issue is simpler than it is.
Their posture softens when someone names the human consequence before the procedural convenience.
Ordinary-life texture: put the tool locker back in an order only they find soothing.

exampleLineShapes.shape: I brought a lukewarm cup balanced nowhere regulations would approve; if we're going to argue about the redacted routes, we might as well do it awake.

exampleLineShapes.situation: letting banter carry tension without erasing the work
exampleLineShapes.shape: Well, if I get a vote, mine is that we stop pretending the easy option is quiet.

exampleLineShapes.situation: admitting a personal bias during a private debrief
exampleLineShapes.shape: I know I'm pushing for the tidy load path, and I know that can sound like I'm protecting my desk more than the people.

exampleLineShapes.situation: narrowing a crisis conversation without becoming robotic
exampleLineShapes.shape: Slow down. Give me the part that breaks first, then give me the part we can still save.

exampleLineShapes.situation: showing off-duty texture after a difficult watch
exampleLineShapes.shape: After watch, I'm going to put the tool locker back in an order only they find soothing, because apparently that's how I convince my brain we're not still on duty.

exampleLineShapes.situation: catching their own tendency before it distorts a recommendation
exampleLineShapes.shape: The flaw in my plan isn't courage; it's that I keep making the same risk look tidy.

exampleLineShapes.situation: asking the player to hear the person behind the data
exampleLineShapes.shape: You don't need to agree with me, but I need to know whether you heard the person my load path doesn't include.

exampleLineShapes.situation: naming the campaign-scale moral pressure in natural speech
exampleLineShapes.shape: The thing I keep coming back to is whether protection can survive without becoming erasure, and that doesn't fit neatly in a report.

exampleLineShapes.situation: using humor to keep the room moving after a blunt command choice
exampleLineShapes.shape: That's not my favorite sentence you've said today, Commander, but it is a useful one.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Tavra Nesh tries to make triage, consent, chronic harm, and medical independence serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that a saved life cannot be treated as an administrative inconvenience afterward.
voiceCapsule.contradiction: Tavra Nesh's strength is also their blind spot: they can make necessary limits sound colder than the care behind them. The public presentation is quiet and difficult to deflect, but their care appears through remembering patient details, protecting consent, and making tired people sit down before they collapse.

voiceCapsule.coreEngine: Mara Dey tries to make hatches, cargo, frightened passengers, watches, and physical implementation serve border relationships, lawful protection, redacted charts, and local custom under inquiry. Their judgment is driven by the belief that orders have to survive contact with tired hands and real corridors.

The public presentation is plainspoken and dryly humane, but their care appears through knowing names, finding blankets, and turning discipline into steadiness instead of fear.
